summary:
Skin-only involvement is insufficient by itself; larger tumors (>4 cm), chest wall invasion, and multifocal disease define LABC according to standard clinical criteria. The presence of just skin symptoms typically requires additional deep tissue infiltration before qualifying a case as LABC. Thus,
\boxed{\text{D}} remains correct based solely upon meeting these comprehensive standards. Agent1 concludes their summary emphasizing the necessity of deeper tumor penetration alongside specific local signs such as size over 4 centimeters, direct chest muscle impact, multiple sites within breasts, thereby ruling out isolated cutaneous manifestations from classifying definitively into this category of aggressive cancers needing immediate intervention strategies tailored towards eradicating both primary lesion alongwith potential metastatic spread via lymph nodes etc., ensuring optimal patient outcomes post-treatment follow-ups too. 

End Summary. To ensure brevity while retaining essential details:

Key Points Summarized:
Locally Advanced Breast Cancer involves large tumors (\>4cm) &/or chest wall/multifoci involvement but NOT mere skin changes which must extend deeply beneath surface layers to be classified accordingly. Therefore,\(\boxed{D}\). End Simplified Version.
